forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of the Bight will drift east on Monday to merge with the strong high in the Tasman Sea. The strong high will extend a ridge across South Australia through to Friday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural areas. Partly cloudy about the coasts, mostly sunny inland. Maximum temperatures slightly below average. South to southeasterly winds with afternoon seabreezes.
Fine apart from isolated showers about central and eastern districts, clearing early evening. South to southeasterly winds extending throughout.

Mostly sunny. Possible early morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures above average about the agricultural area grading to slightly below average in the far north. Northeast winds tending southeasterly about coastal districts.
Mostly sunny. Possible early fog in the southeast. Maximum temperatures above average. East to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Mostly sunny. Maximum temperatures well above average. Northeast to northerly winds, fresh in the west during the afternoon.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Thursday: Generally less than 1 mm for the rest of today with no rainfall expected for the Monday to Thursday period.
